symptoms.symptom_name: Cyclical central cramping with menses, post-menarche onset, prostaglandin GI symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cyclical central menstrual cramping with post-menarche onset and prostaglandin GI symptoms is typical of primary dysmenorrhoea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproductive-age female with acute lower abdominal pain, pregnancy unconfirmed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pregnancy test is mandatory to exclude ectopic pregnancy — an unexcluded pregnancy is the dominant safety consideration.

symptoms.symptom_name: No migratory RLQ / lateralised / fever / discharge features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of lateralising/peritoneal/PID features lowers the probability of these emergencies (examination/imaging still needed if any doubt).

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory to exclude ectopic pregnancy in a reproductive-age woman with acute lower abdominal pain.
If pregnant, or if torsion/other pathology suspected — via in person/ED.
